incentive.statement: Extend the proven Canvas choice-frame cutover to the two adjacent exact-quantity routes without broadening into unrelated failures.

coordinator.responsibility: Keep the slice to Canvas sections 25.6 and 25.8 and preserve the post-miqui assertion baseline.

outcome: Canvas owns the user-confirmed canonical temō-huiā and tēmī-tiā realizations through two canonical choice frames with no surface-overlay identities; OCR and scan interpretation do not normalize their quantity.

responsibilities.statement: Compile both printed Canvas relations while marking their licensing bridges as engine-derived rather than printed.

actors.contribution: Own rule identity, exact realization, bridge provenance, and validation.

responsibilities.statement: Reuse the existing choice-frame application and UI consumers without route-specific reconstruction.

actors.contribution: Carry the compiled choice object unchanged through controls, result, explanation, and dropdown.

responsibilities.statement: Replace stale quantity and overlay-ID expectations with choice continuity and hostile spelling proof.

actors.contribution: Prove the three bounded assertion repairs and no new focused failures.

evidence.result: Both canonical choices pass engine and application validation, retain one identity through controls/result/explanation, and reject short-vowel target clones.
The adjudication handoff's integrated regular suite passed 3496/3496 assertions across 69 suites.

closure.nextLessonEntryPoint: No remaining quantity-route decision; select a new explicit target.
